description: A specialized museum showcasing the history and culture of the Six Dynasties, featuring a strong sense of modern architectural design.
A commercial street district with a Republic of China-era charm, offering dining, entertainment, and shopping; its vibrant nightscape makes it a popular gathering spot for young people.
A dual-purpose railway and highway bridge independently designed and constructed by China, serving as a city landmark.
The mother river of Nanjing; night cruises on traditional boats offer views of ancient architectural complexes along both banks.
A famous snack street in Nanjing, gathering authentic local delicacies and recommended by locals as a must-visit food destination.
Showcasing the intangible cultural heritage of Nanjing Yunjin, with opportunities to observe the craftsmanship of Yunjin weaving.

attraction_name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Music Stage in Zhongshan Scenic Area
description: An open-air music stage built during the Republic of China period, featuring exquisite architecture and a serene, elegant surrounding environment.
A double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and the city skyline.
A sacred site commemorating the Chinese New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now serving as the National Revolutionary Martyrs' Cemetery; it boasts a tranquil environment with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic feature.
